strands.title: PHASE 1 EXECUTION — CRASH RESOLVED, ROBUSTNESS VALIDATED (CYCLE 53)
strands.content: CYCLE 53 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 execution resumed after Cycle 52 diagnosis. CRASH ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED & FIXED: Haiku was returning strands in unexpected shape (likely strands as strings or mixed types in some cycles). TypeError at projector.py:171 during tuple comprehension attempting s['title'] when s was not dict. FIX DEPLOYED (turn 158): Refactored _parse_projection() with helper functions _parse_strand() and _parse_loss() that validate input type before field access. Parser now: (1) checks isinstance(s, dict) before processing strand, (2) filters None results from generator, (3) handles key_claims as potentially malformed array, (4) casts all string/numeric fields through str()/float() to tolerate edge cases, (5) handles instructions_for_next as either string or list (observed in output). OUTCOME: Phase 1 re-execution completed successfully through cycle 4+ without TypeError. HEALTH SNAPSHOT (cycles 1-4): Truth trajectory 0.72 → 0.76 → 0.78 → 0.80, showing stable epistemic growth. Strands count stable at 4. Declared losses cycling 6 → 7 → 3 → 4 (variance expected, losses declared when context pressures spike). Open questions accumulating naturally (10 → 11 → 13 → 14), indicating unresolved branches being tracked. Token footprint compact: ~2500-3300 tokens/cycle, well within budget. PROJECTOR ARCHITECTURE NOW ROBUST: Parser can tolerate malformed strands from LLM without crashing. This enables Phase 1 to extend to full 55 turns without parser bottleneck.

key_claims.text: Haiku was returning strands in unexpected shape (strings, mixed types); TypeError occurred at strands parsing.
Root cause fixed: _parse_projection() refactored with _parse_strand() and _parse_loss() helpers that validate type before field access.
Parser now casts fields through str()/float() and filters None results, tolerating edge cases in LLM output.
Phase 1 re-execution cycles 1-4 completed successfully without TypeError; truth trajectory 0.72 → 0.80 shows healthy epistemic growth.
Declared losses (6 → 7 → 3 → 4) and open questions (10 → 11 → 13 → 14) track context pressure and unresolved branches correctly.
Token footprint compact at ~2500-3300 tokens/cycle; Phase 1 can now extend to full 55 turns without parser bottleneck.

strands.title: PHASE 1 EXECUTION SANITY — EARLY TRAJECTORY ASSESSMENT (CYCLES 1-4)
strands.content: CYCLE 53 INTEGRATION: Early trajectory from Phase 1 (cycles 1-4) shows HEALTHY pattern. EPISTEMIC GROWTH (T/I/F): Overall truth climbs from 0.72 (cycle 1) → 0.76 → 0.78 → 0.80 (cycle 4). Pattern indicates confident claims accumulating as context densifies. Indeterminacy (~0.20-0.22) remains stable, suggesting balanced uncertainty tracking. Falsity very low (0.06-0.08), consistent with projector's role (integrating prior + new, not manufacturing false claims). Variance in values suggests LLM is genuinely updating confidence per content, not outputting fixed scores. STRAND STABILITY: All 4 cycles produce 4 strands with consistent titles: (1) Phase 1 execution status + diagnosis, (2) Debug pathway (Cycle 52 paths forward), (3) Open questions (diagnostics), (4) Replay module (parallel track). Strand count stable despite different input lengths (826 chars → 125 → 16 → 16), indicating projector is robust to sparse turns. Strand merging not observed (would indicate oversaturation), nor fragmentation (would indicate loss of coherence). DECLARED LOSSES PATTERN: Cycle 1: 6 losses (context_pressure dominates as initial projection heavy). Cycle 2: 7 losses (diagnostic reasoning adds complexity). Cycle 3: 3 losses (sparse turn, fewer dependencies dropped). Cycle 4: 4 losses (rebuilding context). Loss categories distributed across context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice; no single category monopolizes, suggesting balanced decision-making. OPEN QUESTIONS TRAJECTORY: Growing from 10 → 11 → 13 → 14, expected as new diagnostic branches opened (crash diagnosis) and replay module planning. Questions persist across cycles (not auto-resolved), indicating projector tracks genuine unresolved state, not clearing for false closure.

key_claims.text: Truth trajectory 0.72 → 0.76 → 0.78 → 0.80 shows healthy epistemic growth; confident claims accumulating as context densifies.
Indeterminacy stable at ~0.20-0.22 across cycles, suggesting balanced uncertainty tracking without drift.
Falsity consistently low (0.06-0.08), appropriate for integration task (not manufacturing false claims).
Strand count stable at 4 across cycles despite input length variance (826 → 125 → 16 → 16), indicating robust projector.
No strand merging observed (which would indicate oversaturation) nor fragmentation (loss of coherence); trajectories are stable.
Declared losses pattern (6 → 7 → 3 → 4) correlates with input complexity; loss categories balanced across pressure/bias/choice.
Open questions grow (10 → 11 → 13 → 14) naturally as diagnostic branches open; not auto-resolved, tracking genuine unresolved state.

strands.title: PHASE 1 EXECUTION PATH FORWARD — FULL 55-TURN EXTENSION & DECISION GATES (CYCLE 53)
strands.content: CYCLE 53 INTEGRATION: With projector crash resolved and early sanity checks passed (cycles 1-4), Phase 1 execution can now extend to full session (55 turns). DECISION GATES: (1) EXTEND TO TURN 55 IMMEDIATELY or (2) INTERMEDIATE GATE AT TURN 20? Rationale for turn 20 gate: (a) Confirms projector robustness beyond cycle 4 (cycles 5-20); (b) Provides token growth data mid-session (slope estimate); (c) Allows strand stability check at larger N; (d) Catches any second-order crashes before full run. Rationale for full 55: (a) Crash fixed, no expected remaining issues; (b) Time-efficient (single run vs. gated runs); (c) Full data collection needed for Phase 2 (full-session analysis). CYCLE 53 DECISION: Recommend INTERMEDIATE GATE AT TURN 20 for safety + information gain. Procedure: (1) Extend run_projection.py max_turns parameter to 20, re-run Phase 1. (2) Capture metrics (token growth, strand trajectory, loss distribution). (3) If successful, extend to 55. If crash or drift detected, diagnose. EXPECTED OUTCOMES (turn 20): ~15 JSON files (tensor_cycle_001 through tensor_cycle_015+), T trajectory reaching ~0.85-0.88, strand count stable or slightly declining (13-15 total across all cycles), open_questions peak then stabilize (indicating resolution/closure of early diagnostics). TOKEN BUDGET: Assuming ~2800 tokens/cycle average, 20 cycles → ~56K tokens (modest). 55 cycles → ~154K tokens (within typical session budget). NEXT IMMEDIATE ACTION: Execute Phase 1 with max_turns=20, capture output logs, measure token trajectory, validate no new crashes.

key_claims.text: Projector crash fixed; early sanity checks (cycles 1-4) passed; Phase 1 can extend to full 55 turns.
Intermediate gate at turn 20 recommended: confirms robustness, provides token growth data, enables mid-session diagnostics.
Expected outcomes at turn 20: T trajectory reaching 0.85-0.88, strand count stable/declining, open_questions peak then stabilize.
Token budget for 55 cycles estimated at ~154K tokens (2800 avg/cycle), within typical session limits.

strands.title: PICHAY REPLAY MODULE — PARALLEL TRACK, PROXY.PY CODE REVIEW QUEUED (CYCLE 53)
strands.content: CYCLE 53 INTEGRATION: Replay module work remains UNBLOCKED and should proceed in parallel to Phase 1 extension. Cycle 52 established prerequisites (proxy.py JSONL schema, page fault definition, pager integration). Cycle 53 STATUS: Code review of proxy.py is queued but NOT YET CONDUCTED. Phase 1 crash resolved with higher priority. REPLAY MODULE TASK PIPELINE: (1) PROXY.PY CODE REVIEW (Cycle 53-54, queued): Extract JSONL schema, fields logged per turn, integration points with pager/eval. Target: understand proxy logging before designing replay.py parser. (2) PAGER.PY / EVAL.PY SURVEY (Cycle 54): Map statistics aggregation patterns, page fault detection logic (if any), integration with replay. (3) REPLAY.PY DESIGN (Stage 2, Cycle 54-55): Specify parser API, reconstruction algorithm, page fault detection, stats output. (4) REPLAY.PY IMPLEMENTATION (Stage 3, Cycle 55-56): Code replay.py, integrate tests, validate against baseline logs. CARRY-FORWARD ASSUMPTION: replay module design will leverage insights from Phase 1 execution (how projector handles sparse turns, how open_questions track unresolved state). Parallel execution of Phase 1 (55 turns) + replay design (proxy/pager review) enables cross-fertilization.

key_claims.text: Replay module code review remains queued; proxy.py review is prerequisite for parser design, not yet conducted.
Replay module task pipeline: proxy review (Cycle 53-54) → pager survey → replay design → implementation, in sequence.
Parallel execution of Phase 1 (55 turns) + replay design enables insights cross-fertilization between systems.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details of specific Haiku response structure (e.g., exact JSON blocks from cycles 1-4 before fix). Now resolved, but granular details not preserved.
declared_losses.why: Crash diagnosis was rapid; focus shifted to fix implementation rather than forensic documentation of malformed strands.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Intermediate debugging output from projector crash (terminal output from turn 155 execution attempt), not saved to structured logs.
declared_losses.why: Error occurred during execution; terminal output not captured to file. Would require re-run with explicit logging to recover.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full trajectory data from failed Phase 1 run (cycles 1-4 before crash). JSON files may exist but not analyzed in detail.
declared_losses.why: After crash fix, focus prioritized moving forward to re-execution rather than forensic analysis of partial prior run.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed error handling comparison between old and new _parse_projection(). Old version's failure modes not fully documented.
declared_losses.why: Fix was implementation-focused; detailed analysis of why old code failed in specific Haiku response patterns deferred.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Considerations for how parser robustness might affect Haiku prompt design. Should prompts be weakened now that parser is more tolerant?
declared_losses.why: Current focus is on Phase 1 execution extension, not prompt optimization. This question queued for future cycles.

open_questions: Will Phase 1 execution at turn 20 (intermediate gate) complete without new crashes? What will token growth slope be?
Do declared losses stabilize or continue variance through turn 20? Is loss distribution (context_pressure vs. traversal_bias vs. authorial_choice) healthy?
Will open_questions peak and then stabilize/decline at turn 20, or continue monotonic growth through turn 55?
At what cycle does the truth trajectory plateau (currently 0.72 → 0.80 in cycles 1-4)? Expected asymptote?
Strand count: will it remain at 4, or will new strands emerge as complexity grows? Any signs of merging?
Token budget: will actual token consumption match estimate (~2800/cycle average)? Any acceleration at later cycles?
After turn 20 gate, will we proceed directly to turn 55, or implement additional intermediate gates?
Proxy.py code review: what JSONL schema does proxy use? Is it compatible with replay.py parser design assumptions?
Page fault definition in pichay codebase: is it string-match re-request, semantic mismatch, or other criterion?
Pager integration with proxy: does proxy pipe through pager, or is pager optional? How do logs reflect pager state?
Can replay module design (proxy review, parser spec) proceed in true parallel to Phase 1 (turn 20 run), or does it block?
Should projector Haiku prompt be refined now that parser is more robust? Risk of over-tolerance vs. improved throughput?
Cycle 53 decision gate: should intermediate analysis (turn 20) include detailed loss category audit, or defer to Phase 2?
Are there known second-order bugs in run_projection.py (e.g., file I/O, session state) that won't emerge until later cycles?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 53-54 IMMEDIATE: PHASE 1 EXTENSION TO TURN 20 (INTERMEDIATE GATE)",
  "",
  "GOAL: Validate projector robustness beyond cycles 1-4, measure token growth, confirm strand/loss/question trajectories.",
  "",
  "STEP 1: RE-RUN PHASE 1 WITH MAX_TURNS=20",
  "  COMMAND:",
  "    cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./experiments/first_projection_turn20/ \\",
  "      20",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED: Completes without crash; generates tensor_cycle_001 through tensor_cycle_020 JSON files.",
  "  CAPTURE: Log file to ./experiments/first_projection_turn20.log for token tracking.",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 2: ANALYZE TURN 20 TRAJECTORY",
  "  METRICS TO EXTRACT:",
  "    (1) Token growth: plot token_count from each cycle JSON file. Slope estimate (tokens/cycle).",
  "    (2) Truth trajectory: extract overall_truth from cycles 1-20, plot trend.",
  "    (3) Strand stability: count strands per cycle, check for new emergence or merging.",
  "    (4) Loss distribution: aggregate declared_losses by category (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint). Pie chart per cycle or running total.",
  "    (5) Open questions: count per cycle, plot cumulative and per-cycle new.",
  "",
  "  COMMAND (skeleton):",
  "    python3 -c \"",
  "    import json, os",
  "    from pathlib import Path",
  "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt",
  "    ",
  "    cycles = []",
  "    tokens = []",
  "    truth = []",
  "    strand_counts = []",
  "    loss_cats = {'context_pressure': 0, 'traversal_bias': 0, 'authorial_choice': 0, 'practical_constraint': 0}",
  "    questions_per_cycle = []",
  "    ",
  "    for i in range(1, 21):",
  "        fn = f'./experiments/first_projection_turn20/tensor_cycle_{i:03d}.json'",
  "        if not os.path.exists(fn):",
  "            break",
  "        with open(fn) as f:",
  "            t = json.load(f)",
  "        cycles.append(i)",
  "        tokens.append(t.get('token_estimate', 0))",
  "        truth.append(t.get('overall_truth', 0))",
  "        strand_counts.append(len(t.get('strands', [])))",
  "        for loss in t.get('declared_losses', []):",
  "            cat = loss.get('category', 'unknown')",
  "            if cat in loss_cats:",
  "                loss_cats[cat] += 1",
  "        questions_per_cycle.append(len(t.get('open_questions', [])))",
  "    ",
  "    # Plot",
  "    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 8))",
  "    axes[0,0].plot(cycles, truth, marker='o')",
  "    axes[0,0].set_title('Truth Trajectory')",
  "    axes[0,0].set_ylabel('overall_truth')",
  "    axes[0,1].plot(cycles, tokens, marker='s')",
  "    axes[0,1].set_title('Token Growth')",
  "    axes[0,1].set_ylabel('tokens/cycle')",
  "    axes[1,0].plot(cycles, strand_counts, marker='^')",
  "    axes[1,0].set_title('Strand Count')",
  "    axes[1,0].set_ylabel('# strands')",
  "    axes[1,1].plot(cycles, questions_per_cycle, marker='d')",
  "    axes[1,1].set_title('Open Questions per Cycle')",
  "    axes[1,1].set_ylabel('# questions')",
  "    plt.tight_layout()",
  "    plt.savefig('./experiments/first_projection_turn20_analysis.png')",
  "    print(f'Token avg: {sum(tokens)/len(tokens):.0f}, slope: {(tokens[-1]-tokens[0])/(len(tokens)-1):.1f}/cycle')",
  "    print(f'Truth trend: {truth[0]:.2f} → {truth[-1]:.2f}')",
  "    print(f'Loss categories: {loss_cats}')",
  "    \"",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 3: SANITY CHECK DECISION GATE",
  "  PASS CRITERIA (all must be true):",
  "    ✓ No crashes in cycles 1-20",
  "    ✓ Token growth reasonable: 2500-4000 tokens/cycle, no explosion",
  "    ✓ Truth trajectory: monotonic increase or plateau, no collapse",
  "    ✓ Strand count: stable (within ±2) or naturally declining (no fragmentation)",
  "    ✓ Declared losses: no single category >60% (indicates imbalance)",
  "    ✓ Open questions: growing but not exponential (not >30/cycle)",
  "",
  "  IF ALL PASS: Proceed to STEP 4 (extend to turn 55).",
  "  IF ANY FAIL: Document anomaly, diagnose before extending.",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 4: EXTEND PHASE 1 TO FULL 55 TURNS (if gate passed)",
  "  COMMAND:",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./experiments/first_projection_full55/ \\",
  "      55",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED: Completes within 5-10 minutes, generates tensor_cycle_001 through tensor_cycle_055.",
  "  TOKEN BUDGET: ~154K tokens (2800 avg * 55), acceptable within session.",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 5: CAPTURE FULL TRAJECTORY (after turn 55 completes)",
  "  ANALYZE: Extend metrics from STEP 2 to full 55 cycles.",
  "  TARGET: Final truth asymptote, token slope through end, strand emergence/merging patterns, loss stability, open_questions resolution.",
  "",
  "  COMPARISON: Turn 20 vs. Turn 55 trajectories to identify second-order effects (e.g., token acceleration, strand fragmentation).",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "PARALLEL TRACK (CYCLE 54): PROXY.PY CODE REVIEW (unblocked)",
  "  GOAL: Extract proxy.py JSONL schema before turning to replay.py design.",
  "  COMMAND:",
  "    grep -n 'class \\|def \\|self.log\\|json.dumps' /home/tony/projects/pichay/src/pichay/proxy.py | head -50",
  "    # Extract key methods, logging patterns",
  "",
  "  DELIVERABLE: Document proxy JSONL schema (fields logged per turn) and integration with pager/eval.",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "CYCLE 53-54 CHECKPOINT:",
  "  ✓ Phase 1 extension to turn 20 EXECUTED",

  "  ✓ Sanity check gate PASSED (or remediation underway)",

  "  ✓ Proxy.py code review STARTED (parallel to Phase 1 extension)",
